question_prompt: What is energy described as in economic growth?
source_text: Electricity and other energy services are major factors in economic and social development. Energy is a key productive asset in economic growth, together with machinery, land, natural resources, and human capital. Access to clean and reliable electricity improves household welfare and creates income-generating opportunities. Affordable electricity allows homes, farms, workshops, schools, and clinics to use lighting, refrigeration, computers, pumps, and machinery more effectively. In rural areas, sustainable electricity can reduce post-harvest losses, support irrigation, improve healthcare, and strengthen education. A village clinic with reliable power can store vaccines and use diagnostic equipment. A school with electricity can provide evening study, digital learning, and better classroom conditions. Although electricity alone is not sufficient for development, it remains a necessary condition for economic and social progress.
correct_answer: Energy is described as a key productive asset in economic growth.
explanation: The first two sentences state this directly.

question_prompt: The electricity planning problem in many developing ____ is more complicated than in developed countries. The challenge is not only to substitute investment cost reductions in electricity use for ____ in power plants, ____ also to obtain financing for electricity-related investment. Power development is difficult ____ electric power industries are among the ____ capital-intensive in an economy, draining scarce ____ resources. And, for countries lacking energy resources, ____ oil and other fuels is also a major drain ____ foreign exchange reserves.
source_text: countries, but, investments, importing, because, most, financial, in
correct_answer: countries
explanation: countries fits the phrase “developing countries.”

question_prompt: Although the weather was bad, she went to work.
source_text: Rewrite using “in spite of”
correct_answer: In spite of the bad weather, she went to work.
explanation: Use a noun phrase after “in spite of,” not a clause.

question_prompt: The salesgirl in this store always welcomes the customers.
source_text: Rewrite in passive voice
correct_answer: The customers are always welcomed by the salesgirl in this store.
explanation: Move the object “the customers” to the front and change the verb to passive.

question_prompt: Mg Mg likes learning English. Ma Ma likes learning English.
source_text: Join using “both ... and”
correct_answer: Both Mg Mg and Ma Ma like learning English.
explanation: Use the plural verb “like” because the subject is plural.

question_prompt: The box is very heavy. I cannot lift it alone.
source_text: Join using “so ... that”
correct_answer: The box is so heavy that I cannot lift it alone.
explanation: Use “so + adjective + that” to show result.

question_prompt: You play tennis.
source_text: Rewrite using “Can”
correct_answer: Can you play tennis?
explanation: Change the statement into a yes/no question with the modal verb.

question_prompt: He said, “Mg Mg, work systematically and carefully if you want to be promoted.”
source_text: Rewrite in indirect speech
correct_answer: He told Mg Mg to work systematically and carefully if he wanted to be promoted.
explanation: In indirect speech, “said” changes to “told” with an object, and the verb becomes infinitive.

question_prompt: She is beautiful like a red rose.
source_text: Rewrite using “as ... as”
correct_answer: She is as beautiful as a red rose.
explanation: Use “as + adjective + as” for equal comparison.

question_prompt: It is necessary for all the citizens to participate in the nation-building tasks.
source_text: Rewrite using “must”
correct_answer: All the citizens must participate in the nation-building tasks.
explanation: “It is necessary to” can be rewritten with “must.”

question_prompt: A lot of rural electrification programs and national electrification agencies have been created in the poorest countries.
source_text: Change into active voice
correct_answer: Governments and development organizations have created a lot of rural electrification programs and national electrification agencies in the poorest countries.
explanation: Add a suitable subject in active voice when the doer is not given.

question_prompt: He worked hard. He wanted to be promoted.
source_text: Join using “to + verb infinitive”
correct_answer: He worked hard to be promoted.
explanation: Use the infinitive of purpose.

question_prompt: Write an argument of not more than 200 words FOR or AGAINST the following statement: “Energy alone is not sufficient for the development of a country.”
correct_answer: Model points should be developed by the learner.
explanation: Learners should state a position clearly, explain it with two or three reasons, and conclude briefly.

question_prompt: Write an essay of not more than 250 words on the following topic: “Benefits of increased electricity access in Myanmar.”
correct_answer: Model points should be developed by the learner.
explanation: A good essay should mention economic growth, health, education, communication, and rural development.

question_prompt: Write a formal letter to the Township Electrical Engineer requesting urgent repair of a damaged distribution line that has caused frequent power interruptions in your area.
source_text: formal letter
correct_answer:
No. 24, Thazin Street,
Monywa
15 July 2026

The Township Electrical Engineer
Electricity Supply Office
Monywa

Subject: Request for urgent repair of damaged distribution line

Dear Sir,

I am writing to request urgent repair work on the damaged distribution line in our ward. For the past two weeks, our area has experienced frequent power interruptions, especially in the evening. As a result, households, shops, and students preparing for examinations have faced serious difficulties.

The unstable power supply has also affected small businesses and water pumping activities. We would therefore be grateful if your department could inspect the line and carry out the necessary repairs as soon as possible.

Thank you for your attention to this matter.

Yours faithfully,

Aung Aung
explanation: The letter uses a formal format, states the problem in the first paragraph, explains the impact in the second, and ends with a polite request.
example: Use formal greeting and polite closing.
